specification.topic-statement: The provider token counting system estimates the token cost of a request by making a synchronous API call to each provider's token counting endpoint, falling back to a byte-based estimator on any failure.

scope.in-scope: The token counting operation for each provider (Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google), the synchronous HTTP call made by each, the fallback to byte-based estimation, and the conditions that trigger each fallback.
scope.boundary:
The byte-based fallback estimator uses a fixed 4:1 bytes-to-tokens ratio. This behavior is covered by the token estimation specification.
When the fallback is triggered, the byte count of a reference string is divided by 4 to produce an estimated token count.
Token counting uses its own synchronous HTTP session (not the async multiplexer used for LLM requests). The async HTTP infrastructure is out of scope here.

behavior.shared-fallback-strategy:
All three providers share the same fundamental fallback strategy: every failure condition produces an estimated token count using the byte-based estimator and returns a success result. Token counting never propagates an error to the caller regardless of the failure.
The fallback activates on any of the following conditions: - Request serialization fails - HTTP session initialization fails - Network error during the HTTP call - HTTP response with status outside the 200–299 range - JSON parse failure on the response body - Missing or non-integer token count field in the response
The byte string used as input to the estimator varies by failure point and provider, but is generally the serialized request JSON body.
The token counting operation always returns a success result. A caller cannot distinguish a successful API count from a silently estimated count.
behavior.anthropic-token-counting:
The Anthropic token counting operation makes a synchronous HTTP POST to the `/v1/messages/count_tokens` endpoint.
The request body is the same as a standard Anthropic Messages API request, but with the `max_tokens` and `stream` fields omitted. The thinking configuration is included if present in the original request. The required headers are `Content-Type: application/json`, `anthropic-version: 2023-06-01`, and `x-api-key` set to the API key. A 30-second timeout is applied.
The response is expected to be a JSON object containing an `input_tokens` field with an integer value. If the field is present and non-negative, it is returned as the token count (truncated to a 32-bit integer).
When the fallback activates after serialization succeeds, the fallback input is the byte length of the serialized JSON body. When the fallback activates because serialization failed, a separate estimator is applied to only the text content of the system prompt and text message blocks (other block types are not counted).
A serialization failure produces an estimate based on partial text content only. A network failure after serialization produces an estimate based on the full serialized body length. These can differ substantially.
behavior.openai-token-counting:
The OpenAI token counting operation makes a synchronous HTTP POST to the `/v1/responses/input_tokens` endpoint.
The request body is serialized in Responses API format regardless of which API mode (Chat Completions or Responses) the model normally uses. The serialization is performed with `stream` set to false. Headers include `Authorization: Bearer {api_key}` and `Content-Type: application/json`. A 30-second timeout is applied. A separate synchronous curl session (not the async multi handle) is used for this call.
The response is expected to be a JSON object with the structure `{"object": "response.input_tokens", "input_tokens": N}`. The `input_tokens` field is extracted as an integer and returned.
When the fallback activates, the byte length of the serialized Responses API JSON body is used as the estimator input. If serialization itself fails, the system prompt byte length is used instead.
Even for models that normally use the Chat Completions API, token counting always serializes in Responses API format and posts to the Responses API counting endpoint. The Chat Completions API does not provide a dedicated token counting endpoint.
behavior.google-token-counting:
The Google token counting operation makes a synchronous HTTP POST to the `{base_url}/models/{model}:countTokens?key={api_key}` endpoint.
The request body wraps the standard `generateContent` request body inside a `generateContentRequest` envelope object. The model field is injected as `models/{model_name}` within this envelope.
Two cases arise: - If the serialized `generateContent` body is 2 or fewer bytes (an essentially empty body), the envelope contains only the model field: `{"generateContentRequest": {"model": "models/X"}}`. - Otherwise, the body JSON is stripped of its leading `{` character and concatenated into the envelope with the model field, producing: `{"generateContentRequest": {"model": "models/X", ...remaining...}}`.
The only header sent is `Content-Type: application/json`. No authentication header is used; authentication is via the `key` query parameter in the URL.
The response is expected to be a JSON object containing a `totalTokens` integer field. If the value exceeds the 32-bit integer maximum, it is clamped to that maximum.
When the fallback activates, the byte length of the envelope-wrapped body (not the original `generateContent` body) is used as the estimator input. If the `generateContent` serialization fails, a byte count of 0 is used.
The body wrapping strips the leading `{` from the serialized JSON and concatenates it into the envelope using a format string. This is syntactically correct for valid JSON but does not parse and rebuild the JSON structure.
